active_question: What is the supplied link for the recommended EMT shears?
edited_answer: The listing is https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0195QI218. Safety cutters should be kept in the rope bag and tested beforehand on the actual rope being used to confirm they cut it effectively.
edited_question: Where is the owner-recommended EMT shears listing, and what cutter-readiness check should accompany it?
paraphrase_rationale: The original answer preserved only the product link. The replacement keeps that exact owner-recommended URL and adds the inspected safety guidance that cutters belong in the rope bag and should be tested on the actual rope.
reason: The original answer preserved only the product link. The replacement keeps that exact owner-recommended URL and adds the inspected safety guidance that cutters belong in the rope bag and should be tested on the actual rope.
support_evidence:
The supplied directory recommends this EMT shears listing for emergency cutting equipment: https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0195QI218.
Crash Restraint identifies safety cutters as the most important non-rope item in a rope bag. Its rope-kit guidance also recommends testing that the chosen cutters work well on the actual rope being used, for example when cutting rope to create shorter lengths.
